title: Share a WIP protocol with a private group
author: Adrien Assie
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qzhdx36

steps.instruction: Create new protocol by clicking on the + sign on the upper right corner of your screen a
Edit name, descriptions, authors, tags and keywords. a
Next to ' Description' , you can click on ' Guidelines and warnings ' and fill the respective box. a

Next to ' Guidelines and warnings' , you can click on ' Materials ' and fill the box: a This will prompt a box to look for specific materials and reagents available online: a The reagent has to be in the database or added with the " Add new " button

Click on steps and then either, add from a text file or click on " New Step " a If you click on new steps you can start adding custom steps a You can group the steps by section, like the (optional) section in this protocol. You can also add fancy graphic depending on what to add to each steps: a
If your protocol is final you can view it and publish it. Or if you want to share it privately with your group before final publish go to step 7
To share a protocol with a group, click share a On the share menu, in the "Start typing group name" box do as it says and look for your private group a Found it, select it and click save
Click on My library, in the top right corner

Find you protocols under My files a Once selected click the protocol (for example in the picture above the one in blue) and drag it to the private group you want to share it with, in this case the Samuel Lab one.
The protocol should now be available to other members of the group to view and edit. When paper with the protocol is published do not forget to make it available to the community.
